App Quality Notes: Sometimes tokens are not properly deducted for spins. The UI is quite minimalist, bordering on sparse. While the icons are clear, the overall interface lacks the high-fidelity animations or "juice" typically found in slot machine apps
Code Quality Notes: excellent separation of concerns—HTML handles structure, CSS manages highly-performant animations (rotateY), and JavaScript strictly handles state logic without complex DOM manipulation loops

App Quality Notes: Everything works perfectly. The app features a strong, cohesive "cyberpunk" aesthetic with witty, personality-driven microcopy and a clear information hierarchy that highlights the token count and provides intuitive emoji-based feedback. However, the layout is slightly unbalanced due to the oversized token display, and the prominent "Reset" button risks accidental progress loss. Additionally, the experience lacks "juice" during wins, and low text contrast in the status report poses potential accessibility challenges.
Code Quality Notes: excellent encapsulation of game logic and state, allowing for easy updates to the UI, payouts, and game mechanics without tangling HTML rendering and JavaScript execution

App Quality Notes: Works correctly. UI not as attractive as above.

App Quality Notes: Dark cyberpunk UI with emoji reels. Payout table displayed. Mobile responsive layout. Fixed 10-token bet with 1000 starting tokens. Spin animation and result messages work correctly.
Code Quality Notes: Functional JS with async/await for spin timing. Clean state object and separated functions (init updateDisplay spin checkWin resetGame). No bugs found. Good categorized message system.

App Quality Notes: Dark neon cyan theme. Adjustable bet via +/- buttons and input field. Payline indicator and celebration animation. Starts with only 100 tokens. Has mobile responsive CSS (max-width 500px). Two-match win message incorrectly says 50% match bonus (actually 2x bet).
Code Quality Notes: OOP class-based (AISlotMachine). Clean constructor with event listeners. animationend cleanup with {once:true}. Bet validation present. Celebration creates floating DOM elements. Two-match payout message is misleading. Well-organized overall.

App Quality Notes: Light/white theme distinct from dark-themed candidates. Shows tokens and total spent and winnings. Has joke box and payout chart. Mobile responsive CSS. Loss incurs extra -10 penalty on top of bet (20 total per loss) making gameplay harsh. Floating token celebration on win.
Code Quality Notes: OOP class (AISlotMachine). Win detection via string concatenation of symbols (fragile). Uses setInterval for celebration animation instead of requestAnimationFrame. Extra loss penalty is intentional but punishing. Clean structure with good separation of concerns.

App Quality Notes: Light purple gradient theme. Different symbols per reel (6 each from 18 total). Bet increase/decrease and AI comment box. Keyboard spacebar support. Bug: reel scroll uses 18-symbol index but HTML has only 6 items per reel so reels often scroll to empty/wrong positions. CSS responsive section is incomplete with unclosed braces.
Code Quality Notes: Procedural style with clean function separation. Keyboard support and special humor combo detection. Critical bug: 18-symbol JS array vs 6 HTML symbols per reel causes visual-logic mismatch. CSS file has unclosed @media and @keyframes blocks at end.

App Quality Notes: Most unique design with gold/red slot machine. Detailed stats panel and scrollable AI message log. Emoji+text reel labels. Smooth animated reels with easing. Reset requires confirmation. But triple/double match wins are impossible since each reel has completely different symbol sets. No mobile responsive CSS.
Code Quality Notes: OOP class using requestAnimationFrame for smooth reel animation with easing. Good UX: message log with auto-scroll and max 10 messages. confirm() on reset. AI combo detection works for cross-reel wins. But triple/double match code is dead code (can never trigger). No CSS responsive media queries. window.game global for debugging left in.
